meaning: n.弃权出赛的马,早就无成功希望的人
examples: The United States is certain to reject the proposal as a nonstarter.
The Senate and the White House said it was a nonstarter.
Any talk of residuals, past or future, was simply a nonstarter.
Krugmanomics was a nonstarter because the gold standard encourages fiscal and monetary discipline, not recklessness.
Such a union, bureaucratically run from Brussels, is ultimately a nonstarter on a continent so utterly diverse as this one.
